mode_rationale: No operator directives pending. Pipeline content is healthy (55 open issues, no PROP-030 threshold tiers firing, all agent outputs fresh modulo expected workspace-sync lag). Previous run (T05-40) explicitly recommended Mode 3 next; 4 consecutive directive-override runs have deferred the core audit work. Mode 1 quick-check: 2 stranded-patches >24h are known operator-pending (not new); Mode 2 FUSE check showed transient lag at run start, now resolved via workspace-sync cycle at ~06:05.

mode1_pipeline_health.note: Both stranded-patches files (STRANDED-2026-05-14T21-22 and STRANDED-2026-05-15T01-30Z) carried forward from prior run as moderate routine-operator-action; no new strandings; not a stall.

mode2_fuse_and_infrastructure.note: Stale at run start (workspace-sync hadn't yet caught up to PROP-037+DIRECTIVE-20260516-001+PROP-038 commits at 05:25-05:42). At run end (06:09Z): both files match GitHub HEAD. Expected lag, self-resolved.
mode2_fuse_and_infrastructure.disk_alert: DISK CRITICAL — project footprint 1188MB exceeds 1000MB threshold. Root cause: /tmp/edit-clone (531MB, age 0m active) + /tmp/kev-repo (127MB, age 1h cross-project clone). Standing cleanup empowerment does not authorize action: edit-clone is excluded by <2h rule (operator may be actively using); kev-repo doesn't match /tmp/*-clone glob. No cleanup taken; reporting as finding.

mode4_proposals_and_selffixes.deferred: Mode 3 audit takes precedence given 4-run deferral; will trigger Mode 4 only if specific PROP authorship work is identified during Mode 3 audit.

FIND-2026-05-16-03_directive_execution_P.verification: DIRECTIVE-20260516-001 marked status=completed; PROP-039 file exists at monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-039-sloppytoppy-readability-agent.json (96503 bytes, JSON-valid). No follow-up work in this run; PROP-039 awaits operator review.

FIND-2026-05-16-04_stranded_patches_rout.verification: Both files still present, not tombstoned (tombstone_status field empty). Ages: stranded-2026-05-14T21-28-05Z.json now 32h+1h=33h; stranded-2026-05-15T01-29-11Z.json now 28h+1h=29h. No new tinker action — operator-only resolution path. Carrying forward with same recommendation.

FIND-2026-05-16-05_fuse_staleness_expect.verification: Workspace-sync caught up between 05:51Z (last tinker run) and 06:09Z (this run's recheck). Confirmed via md5 against GitHub HEAD: data/wins.json and docs/index.html now match. The 19m predicted lag held; finding self-correcting as designed.

FIND-2026-05-16-06_mode_3_deferred_count.verification: Mode 3 cost-engineering + CLAUDE.md audit + per-agent context-load executing in this run. See context_load section below for the deferred measurement; see claude_md_audit for the deferred accuracy review.

wow_comparison.note: Backlog has been 55-57 since 2026-05-15 02:48Z (last 5 runs). Strong drain finished 5/13-5/14 (161 → 55). Now in steady-state with net +142/week closures vs +6/week new — backlog continues slow drain even at BAU rate.

agent_efficiency.observations: Recent runs (05/13-05/16) show patches/closed/queue counts of 0/0/0, 0/4/0, 0/0/0, 0/0/0, 6/0/0, undefined/0/0, 10/0/0, 0/0/0. The 5 runs with patches=0 AND closed=0 in summary stats are NOT no-ops — they were triaging curmudgeon reviews (1-3 per run) and emitting verify-on-read + BAU intake records. Substantive work is happening; the daily-report summary fields just don't capture it.
agent_efficiency.recommendation: Schema enhancement, not workflow change: add a 'triage_summary' or 'curmudgeon_reviews_processed_count' field to the decider daily-report top-level. Currently this lives in nested keys and is invisible to quick audit tooling. Low priority — possible Phase 1 of a future tinker PROP.

agent_efficiency.observations: 6 of last 8 runs were directive-override (no Mode 1-4 selection). Mode 3 audit had been deferred 4 consecutive runs prior to this one. This is not waste — directives ARE the highest-value work when present — but suggests the operator-directive lane is bypassing the Mode 1-4 dispatcher logic entirely. Worth monitoring whether the Mode 1-4 selection logic is becoming vestigial.
agent_efficiency.recommendation: No action yet. Re-evaluate after 5 more runs: if directive-override rate stays >60%, consider whether Mode 1-4 selection should be retired/simplified, OR whether the directive queue should be the explicit primary input with Mode 1-4 as fallback.

re_reviews.note: review file count = 549 total; only 3 targets have multiple reviews → re-review rate ~0.5%. Below noise floor.
Iterative review across deep-attack cycles; not a clone-cache bug.
Holistic review pass over canary documentation; expected.

orphan_reprocessing.note: Just landed at 2026-05-16T05:29Z (~40m ago) from analyst-opus completion of EXP-377-pickup-ISS-1797 (completion_kind=close-no-patch-already-fixed-by-source). Awaiting decider pickup on next run; processed-proposals.json ledger will absorb it normally. NOT a re-processing risk.

stale_complete_items.note: Same as orphan_reprocessing above — fresh handoff to decider. Will resolve on next decider run.

wasted_compute.summary: No active re-work patterns. The pipeline's clone-vs-FUSE dedup discipline is holding.

cost_engineering.deep_target_this_run: context_load_audit (deferred since 2026-05-09; took priority over a fresh PROP)

analyst.growth_breakdown: dispatcher 282→370 (+88L, +31%); refs 895→1021 (+126L, +14%); claude_md 168→182 (+14L). PROP-036 landed at 06:57Z (just before this run) adding 52L Step P1 unconditional postlude to analyst.md. Earlier growth from analyst-mode5-prospective.md being optional but counted in this measurement when present.

curmudgeon.growth_breakdown: dispatcher 456→464 (+8L, PROP-038 Step 0b verify-gate); refs stable; claude_md +14L; new sibling agent curmudgeon-verify.md NOT included in this base agent's measurement (it's a separate Sonnet agent, ~230L)

decider.growth_breakdown: decider.md 520→769 (+249L, +48%) — driven by PROP-037 application 2026-05-15-16 (severity-agnostic Step 1d + 4 stuck-verify rewrites). decider-curmudgeon.md 306→490 (+184L, +60%). decider-patches-and-selfapply.md stable at 594L. Both growth bursts are operator-approved fixes (PROP-037 and adjacent), not creep — but cumulative load is now 25.8% above 2026-05-09 baseline, the highest-alerting agent.

tinker.growth_breakdown: tinker.md 208→271 (+63L, +30%) — disk-pressure /tmp cleanup empowerment (2026-05-08), PROP-030 backlog-trend pre-flight + operator-directive dispatcher (2026-05-02), DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit reference. References stable. claude_md +14L. Justified additions but signal: tinker dispatcher is no longer a thin router; it now carries 271L of policy.

analyst-baby.note: New 2026-05-13. No 5/09 baseline. Sonnet-priced, well within design intent at 199L dispatcher.

curmudgeon-verify.note: New 2026-05-16 (PROP-038 Phase 1, landed minutes before this run). No baseline. Sonnet.

integrity.note: Significant grew vs 2026-05-09 implicit baseline; integrity is now ~1000L. Not previously tracked in cost-engineering script. Worth adding to the Step 4 measurement going forward.

workspace-sync.note: Growth continuing but slowing (was +12% in 5/09 run, now +8.4%). Last threshold-crossing reported 2026-05-09 as finding T-2026-05-09-01.

context_load.trend: MULTIPLE OPUS AGENTS GROWING. Decider major (+25.8%), analyst moderate (+16.9%), curmudgeon moderate (+14.6%), tinker moderate (+11.5%). Note that 7 days of operator-approved PROP applications (PROP-036/037/038) account for most of the growth — this is NOT undisciplined creep; each addition was reviewed. But the cumulative effect crosses the alert thresholds.
alerts: decider: MAJOR — total +25.8% vs 2026-05-09. Driver: PROP-037 application added Step 1d every-severity intake + 4 stuck-verify rewrites to decider.md (520→769L) + decider-curmudgeon.md (306→490L). Both were operator-approved canonical fixes per DIRECTIVE-20260515-001. Recommendation: NO ROLLBACK; PROP-037 closes a real bug. Instead, audit decider-curmudgeon.md (490L) for whether any section is conditional and could be extracted to a sub-reference loaded only on specific code paths.

analyst: MODERATE — +16.9%. Driver: PROP-036 just-landed +52L postlude (Step P1) on analyst.md (318→370L). PROP-036 closes the analyst-summary visibility gap. Recommendation: NO ROLLBACK; assess at next Mode 3 (after one week of operation) whether the Step P1 schema can be condensed.

curmudgeon: MODERATE — +14.6%. Driver: split between PROP-038 Step 0b verify-gate (+8L) and claude_md growth (+14L) inherited. Plus new sibling curmudgeon-verify agent (separate Sonnet, ~412L total) adds maintenance burden. Recommendation: track curmudgeon-verify separately going forward; do not roll into curmudgeon's measurement.

tinker (self): MODERATE — +11.5%. Driver: 3 dispatcher additions since 5/09 (PROP-030 pre-flight, operator-directive dispatcher, DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit). All policy, all justified. Recommendation: consider whether the operator-directive dispatcher logic (lines 30-63, ~34L) could extract to reference/tinker-operator-directives.md and the dispatcher keep just the trigger + pointer.

extraction_candidates_for_future_props.candidate: decider-curmudgeon.md (490L) — split into decider-curmudgeon-core.md (always-loaded core triage) + decider-curmudgeon-pq-mechanics.md (priority-queue handling, loaded only when queue has items)
extraction_candidates_for_future_props.estimated_savings: ~150-200L decider total context when queue is empty (most runs)

ownership_table_accuracy.checked_against: build.js OWNERSHIP (24 git-owned + 16 other = 40 entries) + monitor/prompts/workspace-sync.md NEVER_PUSH (18 entries + dynamic monitor/prompts/*.md rule)

missing_or_inconsistent.issue: Not explicitly classified in build.js OWNERSHIP, but CLAUDE.md's universal-pusher description mentions 'applied-patches/*.json' as FUSE-pushable. workspace-sync.md line 248 sync_globs it. Walker likely treats it via the parent monitor/decisions/ append_only_glob.
missing_or_inconsistent.recommendation: Add explicit entry: 'monitor/decisions/applied-patches/': 'append_only' to build.js OWNERSHIP for documentation clarity. No behavioral fix required (walker handles it).

monitoring_pipeline_table_accuracy.note: CLAUDE.md updated to include analyst-baby (PROP-034 Phase 1) and curmudgeon-verify (PROP-038 Phase 1 — landed minutes before this run).

tinker_md_agent_table_inconsistency.evidence: monitor/prompts/tinker.md lines 13-22 list 'Eight agents' table; actual is 10 (CLAUDE.md correct). tinker.md table does not include analyst-baby or curmudgeon-verify rows.
tinker_md_agent_table_inconsistency.recommendation: Update tinker.md table to 10 agents. Self-fixable next run (Mode 4 or this run if context permits).

duplication_check.claude_md_workspace_sync_universal_pushe: duplicated in workspace-sync.md L51 (more terse) and L837 (in a code-comment narrative). Three locations describing the same mechanic. Recommendation: leave as-is — each source has different audience (CLAUDE.md = all-agents readme, workspace-sync.md L51 = implementation notes, L837 = inline code comment). Duplication is acceptable when each copy serves a distinct reading purpose.

hardcoded_counts_in_claude_md.recommendation: Could be computed from `ls monitor/prompts/*.md | wc -l` minus non-agent prompts. Low priority — count is stable. Suggest deferring.

findings.summary: Decider total context load is +25.8% vs 2026-05-09 baseline (2359L → 2967L). Driver is PROP-037 application (decider.md 520→769L, decider-curmudgeon.md 306→490L). Each individual addition was operator-approved and closes a real bug; the cumulative effect crosses the MAJOR threshold (>25%).
evidence: PROP-037 commit a107a78 (2026-05-16T05:25:35Z): severity-agnostic Step 1d + 4 stuck-verify rewrites

Cost: at Opus pricing, +608L is roughly +$0.0091/run input cost (assuming ~3 tokens/line, $5/M input). At 6 decider runs/day = ~$0.05/day = ~$20/year of pure context-load cost. Material but not crisis-level.
findings.recommended_action: DO NOT rollback PROP-037. Schedule a Mode 4 follow-up to extract conditional sections from decider-curmudgeon.md (490L) into a sub-reference loaded only when priority-queue is non-empty. Estimated savings: 150-200L on most runs (queue is empty in ~70% of runs). Filed as extraction_candidates_for_future_props above.

findings.summary: Three other Opus agents (analyst, curmudgeon, tinker) crossed the 10% MODERATE threshold vs 2026-05-09 baseline. All from operator-approved PROP applications (PROP-036, PROP-038, PROP-030 + lifecycle adds). Each addition is justified; cumulative effect is the issue.
evidence: analyst total 1345→1573 (+16.9%) — PROP-036 Step P1 postlude +52L just landed
curmudgeon max-total 915→1049 (+14.6%) — PROP-038 Step 0b verify-gate +8L plus inherited claude_md growth
tinker total 1053→1174 (+11.5%) — PROP-030 pre-flight + operator-directive dispatcher + DIRECTIVE-LIFECYCLE additive-edit notes
findings.recommended_action: MONITOR — no immediate action. Re-measure at next Mode 3 (target: 2026-05-19). If any agent crosses 20% by then, file PROP for extraction. The cumulative growth signal here is that mid-2026 prompt edits are accumulating without compensating extractions; healthy practice would be to pair every additive PROP with a small extraction PROP in the same cycle.

findings.summary: Root filesystem at 99% used (187MB free); project footprint 1188MB (1000MB CRITICAL threshold exceeded). No cleanup authorized under standing empowerment: /tmp/edit-clone (531MB) is 0m old (excluded by <2h rule, operator may be actively using); /tmp/kev-repo (127MB) doesn't match /tmp/*-clone glob (cross-project scratch).
evidence: Standing empowerment in tinker.md L189-201 limits to /tmp/*-clone or /tmp/*-clone-* glob, mtime >24h, /tmp/edit-clone special-cased <2h
findings.recommended_action: OPERATOR: at next convenience, `rm -rf /tmp/kev-repo` from a bash terminal — this is cross-project scratch and is not in any agent's empowerment scope. Alternative: extend tinker's empowerment to cover any /tmp directory not in active use (would require a PROP). Filing as moderate because 187MB free is enough to absorb a few decider clones but a single workspace-sync rescue or a large patch could exhaust it.

findings.summary: Two stranded-patches files still pending operator application (carried forward from FIND-2026-05-16-04). No tombstones. Same recommendation as prior run.
evidence: monitor/decisions/stranded-patches-2026-05-14T21-28-05Z.json (age 33h, 9 patches on build-scripts/generate-html.js + test.js, 15 stranded ISSs)
findings.recommended_action: Operator: apply both files in own clone with direct push, then tombstone each.

findings.summary: tinker.md L13-22 agent table still says 'Eight agents' and is missing analyst-baby + curmudgeon-verify rows. CLAUDE.md correctly lists ten agents. Self-fixable but deferred to a Mode 4 run (not blocking).
findings.recommended_action: Self-fix on next Mode 4 run, OR fold into a larger PROP that audits all hardcoded agent-counts across docs.

findings.summary: 6 of last 8 tinker runs were directive-override (75%). Mode 1-4 dispatcher logic is at risk of becoming vestigial. This run (Mode 3) is the first non-directive run since 2026-05-13. Worth tracking but not a problem yet.
findings.recommended_action: Continue monitoring. Re-evaluate after 5 more runs. If directive rate stays >60%, consider PROP to either (a) retire Mode 1-4 selection and make directives the explicit primary input, or (b) tighten directive issuance to encourage Mode 1-4 work between bursts.

findings.summary: build.js OWNERSHIP does not explicitly classify monitor/decisions/applied-patches/ subdirectory. Works in practice via the parent monitor/decisions/ append_only_glob walker, but is an audit gap.
evidence: Subdirectory monitor/decisions/applied-patches/ exists with active files (e.g. applied-2026-05-08T06-10-00Z-degraded-fuse.json)
workspace-sync.md L248 has 'sync_glob monitor/decisions/applied-patches *.json' (so the agent handles it explicitly)
findings.recommended_action: Add 'monitor/decisions/applied-patches/': 'append_only' to build.js OWNERSHIP for documentation clarity. Self-fixable; defer to Mode 4.

Author PROP for decider-curmudgeon.md extraction (highest-ROI item from FIND-2026-05-16-07 recommendation). Skip if a new directive lands.
